diagrams.title: 2D Discrete Laplacian Kernels
diagrams.description: Shows the common discrete Laplacian kernels (3x3 convolution filters) used in image processing. Displays both the 4-neighbor and 8-neighbor variants with their weight values.
diagrams.purpose: Explains how the continuous Laplacian operator is discretized for computer implementation, showing the mathematical kernel matrices used in convolution operations

diagrams.title: Laplacian Edge Detection on Sample Image
diagrams.description: Demonstrates the effect of applying the Laplacian operator to a simple image containing shapes with sharp edges. Shows original input, the Laplacian result, and highlights how edges become emphasized.
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes the practical application of Laplacian operator in edge detection, showing how second-order derivatives identify rapid intensity changes

diagrams.title: 1D Signal Second Derivative Visualization
diagrams.description: Shows a 1D signal (like a single row of pixels) with its first derivative and second derivative (Laplacian). Demonstrates how zero-crossings in the second derivative correspond to edges in the original signal.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the mathematical basis of Laplacian: how it measures concavity change and why zero-crossings detect edge locations in 1D, extending to 2D images
